title: TemplateManifest
description: Schema for a tmíxʷ story template (FEATURE_CREEP.md #1). A template is a bundled folder containing this manifest plus its referenced files. The (not-yet-built) loader reads `seed` into a fresh world_state.json and applies `narrator` to the narrative system prompt. NOTE: `seed.bestiary` targets a world_state section that does not exist yet — see templates/README.md.

id.description: Stable kebab-case id; also the folder name.

name.description: Display name shown in the new-world picker.

systemPromptFile.description: Path (relative to the manifest) to the narrator system-prompt override, e.g. system_prompt.md.

promptMode.description: Replace the base narrative system prompt, or append to it.

config.description: Optional per-template narrative gen overrides, merged over config.json.narrative.

gm.description: GM-only reference data. The loader exposes this to the narrator as reference context but NEVER writes it into player-facing world_state.

bestiaryFile.description: Path to a GM-only bestiary JSON (full stat blocks). Reference only.

characterSchema.description: Documentation of the character-card keys the narrator will produce. NOT seeded unless also present in seed.character.

seed.description: Merged into a fresh world_state.json. Keys mirror world_state sections (see core/world_state.js → defaultWorldState).

character.description: Freeform character card. {} means the narrator's onboarding fills it.

session_beats.description: Opening beats as plain strings; the loader wraps them into v3 beat objects (see makeSessionBeat).

bestiary.description: PLAYER-FACING discovered-creature field notes. NEW section — not in the world_state schema yet (see templates/README.md). Seeded empty so play begins with an empty journal the player fills as they meet creatures.

runAtStart.description: Signal to the narrator/loader for the opening flow, e.g. 'character-forge'.
